page1.title: Letters from a Freedom Fighter
page1.subtitle: Quiet paper that carried family love and brave hope.
page1.body:
Long ago, Korea was not free. Brave people fought for their country far from home. Many of them wrote letters to their families.

Some letters were sent from cold prison cells. Some came from far lands across the sea. Each one carried love, worry, and quiet hope.

Ahn Jung-geun wrote to his mother before he died. He asked her to live well and not cry. His last words still feel very warm today.

Other freedom fighters sent letters to their children. They told stories of bravery, kindness, and dreams. Paper became a bridge between hearts and homes.

These letters are not just old paper. They are short poems of family love and courage. When we read them, we feel their <blank>.

questions.stem: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 알맞은 것은?
choices: Letters from freedom fighters carried love and hope to their families.
Old letters are useless because no one can read them today.
Freedom fighters wrote letters only to ask for new clothes.
Letters were the fastest way to travel across the sea.
Korean families never received letters from far lands.

questions.stem: 다음 중 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
choices: Some letters were sent from cold prison cells.
Ahn Jung-geun wrote to his mother before he died.
Freedom fighters sent letters to their children too.
All freedom fighters wrote their letters from home.
The letters carried love, worry, and quiet hope.

questions.stem: 윗글의 밑줄 친 표현 last words 가 뜻하는 것으로 가장 알맞은 것은?
choices: the final letters of the English alphabet
the very last sentences someone says or writes before dying
the closing words of every Korean folk song
long words that take a long time to read
lost words that no dictionary can find anymore

questions.prompt: 다음 요약문의 빈칸 (A), (B)에 들어갈 영어 단어를 본문에서 그대로 찾아 쓰세요.
questions.summary_template: Freedom fighters wrote ____ (A) to their families from far away. The simple paper became a ____ (B) between their hearts and home.

textbook_tieback.body_ko: 5학년 국어에서는 편지글을 읽고 글쓴이의 마음을 헤아려 봐. 일제강점기 독립운동가들이 옥중이나 먼 타국에서 가족에게 보낸 편지에는, 짧지만 깊은 사랑과 용기가 담겨 있어. 안중근 의사는 어머니께 '울지 마시고 잘 살아가십시오'라고 마지막 글을 남겼어. 또 다른 독립운동가들은 아이들에게 정직과 의로움, 꿈을 잃지 말라고 당부했지.

segments.text: for their country far from home.
from far lands across the sea.
to live well and not cry.
stories of bravery, kindness, and dreams.
short poems of family love and courage.

page3.translation_ko: [1] 아주 오래전, 우리나라는 자유롭지 못했어. [2] 용감한 사람들은 고향에서 멀리 떨어진 곳에서 나라를 위해 싸웠지. [3] 그들 중 많은 이들이 가족에게 편지를 썼어. [4] 어떤 편지들은 차가운 감옥에서 보내졌어. [5] 어떤 편지들은 바다 건너 먼 땅에서 왔지. [6] 한 통 한 통에는 사랑과 걱정, 그리고 조용한 희망이 담겨 있었어. [7] 안중근 의사는 돌아가시기 전에 어머니께 편지를 썼어. [8] 그는 어머니께 잘 살아가시고 울지 말아 달라고 부탁드렸지. [9] 그분의 마지막 말은 오늘까지도 아주 따뜻하게 느껴져. [10] 다른 독립운동가들은 자녀들에게도 편지를 보냈어. [11] 그들은 용기와 친절, 그리고 꿈에 대한 이야기를 들려줬지. [12] 종이 한 장이 마음과 집을 잇는 다리가 되었어. [13] 이 편지들은 그저 오래된 종이가 아니야. [14] 가족의 사랑과 용기를 담은 짧은 시 같은 글이지. [15] 우리가 그 편지들을 읽을 때, 우리는 그분들의 희망을 함께 느낄 수 있어.

examples.en: I sent a letter to my friend.
A long letter from grandpa arrived.
It was brave of him to speak up.
examples.ko: 그가 입을 연 것은 용감한 일이었어.

examples.en: Brave kids try new things.
We fight for what is right.
She fights against unfair rules.
He was kept in prison for many years.
examples.ko: 그는 여러 해 동안 감옥에 갇혀 있었어.

examples.en: A cold prison cell felt very lonely.
I carry my book bag every day.
He carried a small box of cookies.
examples.ko: 그는 작은 쿠키 상자를 들고 갔어.

examples.en: Don't let small worry stop you.
Mom carries quiet worry for me.
examples.ko: 엄마는 나를 위해 조용한 걱정을 안고 있어.

examples.en: May I ask you a small question?
She asked me to wait at the gate.
The blanket is soft and warm.
Warm tea helps on cold days.
examples.ko: 따뜻한 차는 추운 날에 도움이 돼.

examples.en: I will send you a card on your birthday.
Please send the email today.
Grandpa loves telling old stories.
Each picture tells a small story.
We crossed the long bridge together.
A bridge connects the two villages.
It takes courage to say sorry.
Her quiet courage helps her friends.

explanations.evidence: 본문은 안중근의 어머니께 보낸 편지, 자녀에게 보낸 편지를 들며 'Each one carried love, worry, and quiet hope'와 'Paper became a bridge between hearts and homes'로 마음을 이어 주는 편지의 가치를 말한다.
rationales: (정답) 독립운동가의 편지가 가족에게 사랑과 희망을 전했다 — 본문 결론.
본문은 'These letters are not just old paper'라며 정반대로 말한다.
본문 'Some came from far lands across the sea' — 받은 적 없다는 건 정반대.

explanations.evidence: 본문은 'Some letters were sent from cold prison cells', 'Some came from far lands across the sea'라며 편지가 감옥과 타국에서 왔다고 한다.
rationales: 본문 'Some letters were sent from cold prison cells' — 일치.
본문 'Ahn Jung-geun wrote to his mother before he died' — 일치.
본문 'Other freedom fighters sent letters to their children' — 일치.
(정답) 본문은 '감옥과 타국'에서 보냈다고 한다. 집에서 보낸 게 아니다.
본문 'Each one carried love, worry, and quiet hope' — 일치.

explanations.evidence: 바로 앞 'Ahn Jung-geun wrote to his mother before he died' 문장이 단서. 죽기 직전에 남긴 마지막 말·글이라는 뜻.
rationales: (정답) 죽기 전에 마지막으로 한 말이나 글 — 본문 맥락 그대로.

explanations.evidence: 본문 'Many of them wrote letters to their families'와 'Paper became a bridge between hearts and homes' 두 문장에서 핵심 명사를 그대로 가져온다.
rationales: (A) letters — 명사. 본문 'wrote letters to their families'에서 그대로.
(B) bridge — 명사. 본문 'Paper became a bridge'의 보어.
